red_team_note: Proposal is same-domain, but rejection may still reduce to collocation knowledge rather than the full end-talks sense.
Arrest is a legal-domain event noun; participants may reject from noun type rather than transfer-of-control meaning.
Rank direction is locally tied to the particle meaning and is comparatively robust.
Station is same-domain, but vehicle vs place knowledge may make the item easier than intended.
Pages are document-domain but not same-class alternatives; rejection may come from comparison-set oddness.
Approval is decision-domain, but the item may still be rejected because it is not an activity or commitment.
Findings are same research domain, but the depleted-resource contrast remains fairly transparent.
Permission is administrative but may be rejected from object transferability rather than PV knowledge.
Ticket is theater-domain, but event vs artifact remains a broad cue.
Lead/loss is same-domain, but the direction of regain may be recoverable from sports semantics.
Invoice is finance-domain, but participants may reject it because it is not an agentive investment or effort.
Terminal is same-domain, but vehicle vs place knowledge may make the item easier than intended.
Testimony is legal-domain, but the place/event-site contrast remains a general cue.
Cut off the topic may be interpretable as ending discussion of a topic; revise before audio.
Consultant/consultation controls domain but still creates a person-vs-event cue.
A report could arguably set out an appendix in a document-organization sense; native review should check acceptability.
Whistle is sports-domain, but rejection may come from broad analyzability.
Patients/symptoms controls domain but retains an animacy/recipient cue.
Brochure can be put up in some display contexts; keep as reserve, not active list.
Defeat is same-domain, but rejection may be recoverable from outcome semantics.
Confusion is research-discourse related, but it is not a claim; cue remains broad.
Receipt is finance/document-domain but the official-service contrast is still transparent.
Witness came out can have literal or identity-disclosure readings; revise before audio.
Evidence is policy/research-domain but may be rejected because it is not an event/result caused by reforms.
Analysis/hypothesis is a strong task-vs-proposition contrast within research discourse.
Condition-vs-symptom contrast is medically local and stable for the exclude-possibility sense.
A review can point out an appendix in some contexts; native review should test whether it is truly unacceptable.
Study/heading contrast is local to summarization and comparatively stable.
Treatment/thermometer controls medical domain, but event-vs-instrument cue remains salient.
Process/footnote contrast is local to information organization and comparatively stable.
Equation/pencil controls classroom domain, but problem-vs-tool cue remains broad.
